overview: Jeskai Lessons is a three-color tempo deck that leverages card advantage and sorcery-speed recursion to control the board while maintaining pressure. By cycling through cheap spells and reusing key threats, the deck aims to outvalue opponents in the mid-to-late game. The inclusion of Gran-Gran and Accumulate Wisdom provides a powerful engine for recurring removal and finishers, allowing you to stabilize against aggressive strategies and grind out control matchups.
win_conditions: Winning via superior card advantage and resource depletion
Recurring key threats and finishers via Accumulate Wisdom and Gran-Gran
Applying consistent sorcery-speed pressure with Stormchaser's Talent and other spells
Outlasting opponents through efficient removal and board control

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction or recursion engines
Mana bases that support three-color casting by turn three
Combinations of Accumulate Wisdom and cheap threats
Hands containing Gran-Gran or early removal
Keep any hand with a land, Accumulate Wisdom, or Gran-Gran plus a land.
Prioritize hands with 2-3 lands and at least one Lessons card or early spell.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or card draw
Mana bases with four or more lands
Hands without a clear path to turn two or three plays
Hands reliant solely on high-cost finishers
Mulligan pure curve-outs, hands with only non-basic lands that enter tapped without fetches, or hands lacking a way to cast Accumulate Wisdom (needs 2U) if you have no cantrips.

early_game: Play lands and cheap interaction to disrupt opponent's early strategy
Hold up mana for potential recursion or counterplay
Establish early board presence with low-cost threats
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip or cheap instant to set up the graveyard.
Turn 2: Play a land, cast Accumulate Wisdom if possible, or cast Gran-Gran and follow up with instants.
If opening with Lessons, tutor for Accumulate Wisdom or a key spell like Stormchaser's Talent and cast it.
Mana base should prioritize fetching basics to ensure consistent WUU drops.
mid_game: Activate Gran-Gran to reuse removal and stabilize the board
Cast Accumulate Wisdom to filter for answers or finishers
Trade efficiently while preserving card advantage
Treat your graveyard as a secondary hand
never cast a spell you can't afford to lose unless it's a win-con or essential interaction.
Use Lessons to find specific answers or Accumulate Wisdom if it's in the graveyard.
Manage mana to keep open for counterspells or removal when needed, but commit to casting spells to fuel Gran-Gran and card draw.
Commit threats early if the board is empty, as Gran-Gran scales with spell volume.
Hold back if facing sweepers or heavy removal
late_game: Leverage recurring threats to overwhelm opponent's resources
Use sorcery-speed spells to dictate the game state
Push for damage while maintaining enough cards to respond to late-game threats
A fully fueled Accumulate Wisdom paired with Gran-Gran and a graveyard full of instants/sorceries creates an unstoppable token storm.
Direct damage spells or combo finishers can close out games against control.
Go all-in when you have Accumulate Wisdom on the battlefield, a full graveyard, and enough mana to cast 3+ spells in a turn.
Also commit when your opponent has no sweepers or graveyard hate in play.

key_cards.name: Stormchaser's Talent
key_cards.role: Provides versatile spot removal and card advantage
key_cards.notes: Use to clear key threats while drawing a card, maintaining resource parity

key_cards.name: Accumulate Wisdom
key_cards.role: Core recursion engine for reusing spells and threats
key_cards.notes: Cast at sorcery speed to return high-impact cards to hand, enabling repeated value

key_cards.name: Gran-Gran
key_cards.role: Enables repeated casting of instants and sorceries
key_cards.notes: Hold up mana to activate its ability and recast removal or finishers, maximizing tempo

sequencing_tips: Prioritize holding up mana for recursion over committing to early plays
Cast Accumulate Wisdom before committing to board state changes
Use Gran-Gran's ability to recast removal after opponent commits resources
Balance aggression with card advantage to avoid running out of answers
Common mistake: Overcommitting to casting spells without considering graveyard hate or sweepers.
Always keep a land in hand to ensure you can cast Accumulate Wisdom if it's in the graveyard.
Stormchaser's Talent synergizes heavily with high spell volume
use it to dig for key pieces or fuel your engine.
Remember that Accumulate Wisdom exiles spells cast from the graveyard, so you can only cast each spell once per game unless you have ways to return it to the graveyard.

matchups.advice: Focus on early interaction and stabilizing with Gran-Gran recursion
Race while maintaining card advantage through repeated removal
Apply consistent pressure and use recursion to outvalue their hand traps

Use split-second spells, cheap cantrips to out-value counters, or cast Accumulate Wisdom in response to their counterspells to protect it. Utilize Lessons to find protection or alternative win conditions.

Apply early pressure with cheap instants and Gran-Gran. Use artifact destruction and graveyard hate to disrupt their mana rocks and recursion. Don't let them stabilize; trade efficiently and punish their overextended mana.

Win the resource war by casting more spells to trigger Gran-Gran and draw cards. Prioritize Lessons to find Accumulate Wisdom or key interaction. Use targeted removal to eliminate their Gran-Gran or Accumulate Wisdom before they can stabilize.

common_threats: Watch for sweepers (e.g., Supreme Verdict, Wrath of God), graveyard hate (e.g., Rest in Peace, Leyline of the Void), and counterspells.
Note when opponents save removal for Accumulate Wisdom or Gran-Gran.
Track their mana development to predict when they can cast their win conditions.
